pattern: Splitting a small array into equal-size groups with no repeated value inside a group is a set-partition problem: precompute every valid group's cost, then bitmask-DP over which elements have been assigned, always filling the lowest still-unassigned slot next to avoid counting the same partition twice.
hints: Every group must have exactly n/k elements with all distinct values, and its cost is max minus min of that group; precompute this cost for every subset of the right size that qualifies.
Think of building the partition bit by bit: a state is which elements have already been placed into some group, and a transition adds one more complete valid group.

To avoid re-deriving the same partition twice (since groups aren't ordered), always grow the state by choosing a group that contains the lowest-indexed element not yet placed; take the DP value at the full mask, or -1 if it's unreachable.

edge_inputs.description: one duplicate pair forces a specific split
several repeats across a larger array
k equals array length, each group is a single element so cost is always zero
k = 1, the whole array is one group
a value repeated more times than k allows makes the partition impossible
all identical values with k > 1 groups of size > 1 is always impossible
distinct values already, so grouping choice still matters for minimizing spread

stress.description: arrays near the maximum size 16 with a random group count that divides evenly
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = 16
    divisors = [d for d in range(1, n + 1) if n % d == 0]
    k = rng.choice(divisors)
    nums = [rng.randint(1, n) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums, k]

oracle_python:
class Solution:
    def minimumIncompatibility(self, nums, k):
        n = len(nums)
        size = n // k
        groups = [set() for _ in range(k)]
        gmin = [None] * k
        gmax = [None] * k
        best = [-1]

        def backtrack(i, used_groups):
            if i == n:
                total = 0
                for g in range(k):
                    if len(groups[g]) != size:
                        return
                    total += gmax[g] - gmin[g]
                if best[0] == -1 or total < best[0]:
                    best[0] = total
                return
            v = nums[i]
            limit = min(used_groups + 1, k)
            for g in range(limit):
                if len(groups[g]) >= size:
                    continue
                if v in groups[g]:
                    continue
                old_min, old_max = gmin[g], gmax[g]
                groups[g].add(v)
                gmin[g] = v if old_min is None else min(old_min, v)
                gmax[g] = v if old_max is None else max(old_max, v)
                nu = used_groups + 1 if g == used_groups else used_groups
                backtrack(i + 1, nu)
                groups[g].discard(v)
                gmin[g], gmax[g] = old_min, old_max

        backtrack(0, 0)
        return best[0]
